betterquesting:10.desc:8:
With that 31/31/31 seed now in hand you will (ideally) §lnever have to complete 
the entire statting process again. §r

Once replicated, you can cross this perfect seed with itself (keep them fertilized!) to access the pools that it has access to! §c§c§4§nThis is the intended path for early game crop discovery. §rWhile it is much more random than deterministic breeding, it will let you collect a wider range of species quicker.

Keep in mind that: when pool breeding occurs, the offspring has a chance to be any crop found within any pools which the parent crops had access to. §lThe mutation selection occurs prior to the check for enviroment. §rIn practice this means that if you're searching for a specific species in say, the Food pool, it will take much longer to appear than one in the Reed pool because there are fewer alternative species. [warn]No matter the environment.[/warn]

This is why deterministic breeding will be the simpler and quicker method for obtaining specific species, once you've got a solid range of others to breed from.
